a_image.alt: Chalkboard style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Educational

sections.a_description: Chalk-white text on a dusty green-black surface, slightly smudged edges, and the nostalgic warmth of a classroom chalkboard — this aesthetic transforms the screen into a slate surface where every word feels hand-written by a patient teacher. The grain of the board, the softness of the chalk, and the slightly uneven letterforms create an atmosphere that's warm, educational, and reassuringly analog.

Chalkboard styling appears on educational platform landing pages, tutoring service sites, and restaurant menu boards. You'll find it on craft brewery taproom displays, school event pages, workshop announcement sites, and any project that wants to feel approachable, hand-crafted, and warmly pedagogical.

A schoolroom chalkboard — dusty green-black surface with chalk-white text that has that characteristic soft, slightly textured quality. Background: deep chalkboard green (#2a3a2a) with a subtle grain texture via pseudo-element to simulate the slightly rough slate surface. The .inner panel has no distinct background — text lives directly on the chalkboard. A thin chalk line (dashed white border at 50% opacity) frames the content area. Chalk dust smudge effect at the bottom via a gradient pseudo-element (white-to-transparent at very low opacity). Text has a slightly rough, chalk-like quality — achieved with text-shadow at very low opacity in slightly offset positions. Set in Caveat for headings — a handwriting font that perfectly captures the loose, slightly uneven quality of chalk lettering on a blackboard. Patrick Hand for body — another handwriting font with more even letterforms for better readability at paragraph lengths. Heading in Caveat at 34px, line-height 1.2, color #e8e0d0 (warm chalk white, not pure white). Eyebrow in Patrick Hand at 14px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #a8c8a8 (faded green-chalk). Body in Patrick Hand at 16px, line-height 1.7, color #d0c8b8. Buttons: primary — chalk outline (2px solid #e8e0d0), no fill, chalk text; hover fills with semi-transparent white. Secondary: thinner chalk border. Alternatives: Indie Flower (bubbly handwriting), Kalam (brush-like), Handlee (casual).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
